heading: DNA identification of missing persons
label: 40726 DNA identification of missing persons
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 14136d of Title 42
Amendments2016—Subsec. (c). substituted “fiscal years 2017 through 2021” for “fiscal years 2005 through 2009”.Pub. L. 114–324
amendments: Amendments2016—Subsec. (c). substituted “fiscal years 2017 through 2021” for “fiscal years 2005 through 2009”.Pub. L. 114–324

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Attorney General shall make grants to promote the use of forensic DNA technology to identify missing persons and unidentified human remains.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: b Requirement
children.text: Each State or unit of local government that receives funding under this section shall be required to submit the DNA profiles of such missing persons and unidentified human remains to the National Missing Persons DNA Database of the Federal Bureau of Investigation.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: c Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2017 through 2021 to carry out this section.
